summary: 2026-07-24 08:30:29 PDT — The drift linter passes all 89 checks, but the branch has one AC5 overwrite violation and three contract inconsistencies affecting determinism, patch replay, and the declared recon boundary.

findings.title: Second-resolution stamp is not collision-proof
findings.claim: The artifact key contains only YYYY-MM-DDTHHMMSS, so two invocations started within the same second resolve to the same path. Line 205 nevertheless says collisions are impossible and that a later invocation can never overwrite an earlier plan, violating AC5's no-overwrite guarantee. The linter only checks that these claims are present, so it passes this broken invariant.
findings.suggestion: Add genuinely unique identity, such as fractional seconds or a nonce, or fail closed when the computed path already exists. Update the linter to reject a collision-prone naming rule rather than merely pinning the claim.

findings.title: Dirty-tree behavior contradicts the stated determinism invariant
findings.claim: The hard constraint promises “Same repo state + same overrides ⇒ same plan,” but lines 72–74 assign dirty trees a fresh wall-clock evaluatedAt and explicitly call them non-reproducible. Recompiling the same dirty content can therefore change the churn window and plan. The linter at line 81 incorrectly requires this overclaim, so a corrected skill would fail while the contradictory skill passes.
findings.suggestion: State the settled AC4 rule precisely: replayability from recorded inputs, with same-source reproducibility guaranteed only for clean trees. Replace the linter assertion with pins for that qualified invariant and the dirty-tree exception.

findings.title: Add patches cannot deterministically reconstruct omissionRisk
findings.claim: The add patch records only lens, altitude, scope, and depth, while claiming omissionRisk derives deterministically through steps 2 and 5. Step 5 merely instructs the agent to write a one-line risk and supplies no table or template mapping row intent to that text. Replaying the same add patch therefore requires fresh judgment and can produce a different canonical plan, contrary to the complete-patch and replayability contract.
findings.suggestion: Either include omissionRisk in the add payload, or define a deterministic omission-risk template/catalog keyed by the recorded row intent. Add a linter pin for the chosen source instead of checking only that the word omissionRisk exists.

findings.title: Schema still claims steps 1–2 provide a Table B tier
findings.claim: The profile description lists “Table B tier” as data carried from /dev-audit steps 1–2, although the settled round-6 change records that Table B is produced at step 5 and removed this unavailable input. This leaves the canonical contract internally inconsistent and invites an invented informational value.
findings.suggestion: Remove “Table B tier” from the profile description and make the linter's absence check cover the schema as well as the skill.
